full_text:
The initial diagnosis was a benign tumor.

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated first using Dispase, followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H). Dispase dissociation media included 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 200 µl Dispase + 100 µl DNAse, and the sample was incubated at 37C rotating for 30 min, vortexed before and after. Suspension was filtered through 70 µm cell strainer.

The leftover pieces were further dissociated with C&H. The C&H dissociation media included 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 1 ml C&H+ 100 µl DNAse. The sample was incubated at 37C rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. After incubation with C&H, the suspension was filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

For both cell suspensions (dispase dissociated and dispase+C&H dissociated) RBC lysis with 2 ml for 5 min + 10 ml DMEM/F12+++, and the final pellet was resuspended in 1 ml each. From both, a 1:10 dilution was made for counting.

Cell counts:

From dispase+C&H dissociated cells, took 1.5 E6 cells aside, and continued to MACS with the rest (12.9 E6 cells).

Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)

Anti-fibroblast MicroBeads protocol by Miltenyi Biotec was used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA) to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before using it.

Centrifuged the cells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C. The pellet was resuspended in 103.2 µl of cold MACS buffer (commercial protocol suggests 80 µl per 10 E6 cells). Added 25.8 µl of Anti-Fibroblast MicroBeads to the cell suspension (protocol: 20 µl per 10 E6 cells) and incubated 30 min at RT. Washed the cells with 2 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 300 g for 10 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice. The beads were kept sterile and therefore worked inside a laminar hood until the incubation.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, wash right before starting. This time first washed one column, but since that needed to wait for the cell suspension, washed a new column. After washing the newer column, applied the cell suspension onto the column, and then, washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS buffer. Kept the same 15 ml Falcon tube under both of the columns, so when doing the pre-washing twice, the actual sample, and all post-washings, the falcon got full. Collected the last wash (3 ml) to a 50 ml falcon tube and after that pooled all flow though to that 50 ml falcon tube.

Remember to always wait until all liquid has entered through the column.

The column was removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly pushing the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed out.

Both cell suspensions were centrifuged 300 x for 10 min and then resuspended in 1 ml FACS buffer. Counted the cells, and cell counts:

Flow cytometry

Prepared the following samples:

unseparated, multicolor: 1 E6 cells

cells were taken from the initial cell suspension before MACS

column bound, multicolor: 0.91 E6 cells

flow-though, multicolor: 1 E6 cells

DCM-FMO: 1 E6 cells

0.5 E6 cells from unseparated cell suspension and 0.5 E6 cells from flow-through cell suspension

FAP-FMO: 1 E6 cells

taken from flow-through cell suspension

Stained with fibroblast panel, and according to the flow cytometry staining part of the Dissociation optimization protocol https://docs.google.com/document/d/1n5MOSWOqN9LEyI8HYTw-o3bh4YMAGmki/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=101237607924871522883&rtpof=true&sd=true
